MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-10-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via CourtCall:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff;
Emily D. Bergstrom of BHC Law Group LLP (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant M. Slayen and Associates, Inc.

Ruling:
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant M. Slayen and Associates, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant's notice of motion states that the motion is based on affirmative evidence, which establishes, affirmatively, that Plaintiff was not exposed to an asbestos-containing product for which Defendant may be liable. Defendant has failed to shift the burden on this ground. Based on Plaintiff's deposition testimony, which is the only evidence Defendant offers, Defendant has not shown that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that he was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Plaintiff testified that he came into contact with one of Defendant's employees on board the Manley for 10 to 15 minutes. (Rose Declaration, Ex. C at 536:18-537:25; 540:16-541:6.) Defendant also concedes that one or more M. Slayen employees worked aboard the five other auxiliary ships for which Plaintiff was in charge of hiring contractors to perform work. (Defendant's Separate Statement, Facts 3 and 4.) Plaintiff's testimony that he did not remember hiring Defendant to do any of the contracting work is insufficient as Defendant failed to show that Plaintiff would not have been able to recognize the Defendant had he been shown Defendant's invoices at the deposition. (Bergstrom Declaration, Ex. F at 239:10-25; see also Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2007) 143 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1439; Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal. App. 4th 64, 81 (courts "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered").) Though the Court need not get to Plaintiff's evidence as the burden did not shift, Defense counsel acknowledged that she produced copies of the invoices to Plaintiff on April 20, 2021, three months before his deposition. (Bergstrom declaration on reply at paragraphs 2-3.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee; COURT REPORTER via CourtCall: Janice Dispo, CSR #13299 of Veritext (510)451-1580, email: jdispocsr@gmail.com (503/CML)

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(a). Opposition filed by Defendant M. Slayen and Associates Inc. The Court did not consider the joinder by Honeywell International Inc. for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7B.) Plaintiffs have shown that Roy Grigsby's health is such that a preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing his interest in the litigation.

The trial date is November 15, 2021 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is November 29, 2021. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is November 10, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is October 29, 2021. Expert discovery cut-off date is November 12, 2021. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than August 27, 2021. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
